canonical_label: Forrester, P.J. & Witte, N.S. (2002) Application of the τ-function theory of Painlevé equations to random matrices: P_V, P_III, the LUE, JUE, and CUE. Comm. Pure Appl. Math. 55(6):679-727.
resolution_method: Crossref API bibliographic search by author + title; arXiv abstract page verification

primary_record.title: Application of the τ-function theory of Painlevé equations to random matrices: P_V, P_III, the LUE, JUE, and CUE
primary_record.journal: Communications on Pure and Applied Mathematics
primary_record.verification_evidence: Crossref returned exact title match 'Application of the τ-function theory of Painlevé equations to random matrices: P_V, P_III, the LUE, JUE, and CUE'; authors {Forrester, Witte}; CPA vol 55(6):679-727; print-year 2002 (online 2002-03-27); only Forrester-Witte paper with print-year 2002 explicitly treating P_III tau-function; matches arXiv:math-ph/0201051 abstract verbatim

arxiv_preprint.verification_evidence: arXiv abstract page returned matching title verbatim, authors P.J. Forrester + N.S. Witte, abstract identical to Crossref CPA record abstract

disambiguation_from_other_forrester_witt.rejection_reason: Print-year 2001, not 2002. Treats P_IV/P_II/GUE; does not include P_III which is the equation flagged at 058R phase_b_canonical_map L136-140.

Print-year 2004; treats P_VI not P_III. Submitted to arXiv 2002-04 but published only in 2004. 069r1 'Forrester-Witte 2002' year-stamp matches arXiv submission of math-ph/0201051 (CPA 2002), not this paper.
Year 2003; topic is discrete Painlevé, not the continuous P_III tau-function relevant to 069r1 chart-map.

Three-author paper (Witte, Forrester, Cosgrove); short summary note (10 pp), not the full τ-function development. Published 2002-07. While year-matched, it lacks the substantive content (full P_III/LUE characterization) needed by 069r1.

discrepancies_with_069r1_cite.actual_finding: The 069r1 year-stamp '2002' is satisfied by exactly one substantively-relevant Forrester-Witte paper: 10.1002/cpa.3021 (CPA 55(6), 2002) treating P_V/P_III/LUE/JUE/CUE. arXiv preprint math-ph/0201051 dated 2002-01-24. The companion P_IV/P_II/GUE paper (10.1007/s002200100422, CMP 2001) and P_VI paper (10.1017/s0027763000008801, Nagoya 2004) are flanking but year-mismatched.
discrepancies_with_069r1_cite.recommendation: Synth at LANE-1 should canonicalize 069r1 'Forrester-Witte 2002' → DOI 10.1002/cpa.3021 (arXiv:math-ph/0201051). The full Forrester-Witte tau-function series spans 4 papers (CMP 2001 → CPA 2002 → Nagoya 2004 → SAPM 2017); for chart-map closure at A.1.5.F1 only the CPA 2002 paper is strictly required.

accessibility_tier_definition: Tier 1 = open-access via arXiv preprint (free PDF, no acquisition action required)
accessibility_evidence: arXiv:math-ph/0201051 PDF freely accessible at https://arxiv.org/pdf/math-ph/0201051 under arXiv assumed-1991-2003 license. Wiley CPA published version (10.1002/cpa.3021) is paywalled (Tier 3) but content is verified equivalent to arXiv preprint per Crossref abstract match. Full 49 KB AMSLatex source also available via https://arxiv.org/src/math-ph/0201051.
estimated_acquisition_cost_usd: 0 (arXiv preprint is open-access; published Wiley version costs ~35-50 pay-per-view but content equivalent)

relevant_sections_for_069r1_F1_chart_map: §2-3 Okamoto P_V/P_III τ-function development (canonical chart variables explicit)
§4 hard-edge LUE scaled limit → P_III tau-function characterization
§6 difference equation (Schlesinger transformation) for the τ-function
primary_substrate_gap_target.rationale: Forrester-Witte 2002 explicitly establishes the (α, β, γ, δ)_{P_III} ↔ (a, b, c, d)_{Hamiltonian} ↔ (τ-function arguments) chart correspondence that 069r1 058R phase_b_canonical_map L136-140 flags as the substrate gap. The paper IS the canonical reference for this specific bridge.
primary_substrate_gap_target.sufficiency_for_chart_map: Forrester-Witte 2002 alone, when combined with Okamoto 1987 (Funkcial. Ekvac. 30:305 for P_III, already in 069r1 substrate), should close the A.1.5.F1 substrate gap on the random-matrix-side. The complementary Jimbo-Miwa-Ueno isomonodromic-deformation viewpoint (Part II) is independent confirmation but not strictly required if FW 2002 + Okamoto 1987 are accepted as substrate.
